page: Microsoft Azure F80as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Fasv7 family. It features 80 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 320.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark data places the F80as_v7 in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and memory metrics. The AMD EPYC processor architecture, which supports advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI, enables high-throughput operations. This is reflected in the top-tier memory bandwidth results, reaching 14130.92 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 476.73 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in static web serving throughput at 110.69 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 203.02 million operations per second.

For machine learning and text processing workloads, the F80as_v7 achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds on small and medium LLM models. Large model execution remains strong, falling within the top 25% of tested configurations. The absence of a dedicated GPU means these workloads rely entirely on CPU-bound execution, which is accelerated by the processor's vector and cryptography instruction sets. This configuration is best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, Redis caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure F80as_v7 is an x86_64 server from the Fasv7 family, featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz and 320.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this server lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached Premium Storage. It delivers top-tier performance across stress-ng CPU benchmarks, PassMark metrics, and memory bandwidth tests, with cached read speeds reaching 14130.92 GB/sec. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, driving top-tier results in Redis operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F80as_v7 is optimized for compute-intensive, non-GPU workloads such as database operations, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80as_v7 is an x86_64 server featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz and 320 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80as_v7 is an x86_64 server with 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 320 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eighty-core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory allocation, engineered for high-throughput compute and database workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
320.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512 and cryptography hardware acceleration flags
Requires network-attached Premium Storage due to lack of local drives
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, databases, and CPU-based inference
